steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must complete a logical inference. The key facts: (1) neither Geoffrey of Monmouth’s History of the Kings of Britain nor any work predating it mentions the Round Table; (2) Malory’s Le Morte d’Arthur does include it. Since many of Malory’s sources derive from Geoffrey, but the Round Table appears nowhere in Geoffrey’s tradition, the blank must explain where Malory found it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Geoffrey of Monmouth’s accounts … are more similar overall in content to Perceval than to Le Morte d’Arthur.’: The passage never compares Geoffrey to Perceval; this comparison isn’t supported. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Malory encountered the Round Table in a source that Geoffrey of Monmouth was not familiar with when writing his History.’: This fits exactly — if Geoffrey’s sources lack the Round Table and Malory has it, Malory must have drawn from a source outside Geoffrey’s tradition. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Le Morte d’Arthur is more historically accurate than History…’: Historical accuracy is never discussed; and the reasoning given (that Perceval hadn’t been written yet) doesn’t follow from the evidence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘… Malory preferred to include Geoffrey of Monmouth’s version…’: This contradicts the evidence — Malory included the Round Table, which is absent from Geoffrey’s version. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
